bidding.note: S 4=5=2=2 15 bal answers 3NT (15-17, no shortness) correctly; cue/RKCB tail signs off in 5H.
S 3=5=4=1 answers 3C (singleton C); partnership cue-bids and RKCBs to a sound 7H making 13.
S 4=5=4=0 answers 3C (void C); slam-try to 6H making 12. Clean shortness ask.
S 5=1=4=3 answers 3H (singleton H) correctly; long cue/RKCB tail then signs off in 5S off a keycard.
S 5=4=3=1 answers 3C (singleton C); RKCB reveals missing keycard, signs off 5S. Clean ask, long tail.
S 6=1=4=2 answers 3H (singleton H); RKCB finds a key missing and stops in 5S.
S 6=2=1=4 answers 3D (singleton D); cue and RKCB to 6S making 12. Shortness on clear display.
S 5=3=2=3 14 bal: 3NT (no-shortness) is light; 4S minimum also fits. N drives to 6S, makes 12.
S 5=3=5=0 has a void C and good 5-card D but answers 4S (minimum, no shortness) — suppresses the void. Indefensible answer.
S 3=5=1=4 has a singleton D but answers 4H (minimum, no shortness) — fails to show the shortness Jacoby asks for.
S 5=3=3=2 14 bal answers 3NT (no shortness); cues fail to find slam values, signs off 4S.
S 5=3=3=2 answers 3NT correctly; long RKCB tail pushes to 7S — aggressive grand (DD down). 6S the safe call.
S 5=2=3=3 13 bal answers 4S (minimum, no shortness); N passes. Clean minimum signoff.
S 5=2=3=3 answers 4S (minimum); N has the values, RKCBs to 6S making 12.
S 5=4=2=2 12 answers 4S (minimum); N drives RKCB to 6S making 12. Clean.
S 2=5=4=2 12 bal answers 4H (minimum, no shortness); N passes. Textbook minimum signoff.
S 3=5=3=2 answers 3NT correctly; N drives to 6H on ~29 combined — thin (DD down). Stopping in game defensible.
S 6=4=2=1 answers 3C (singleton C); cue and RKCB to 6S making 13. Clean shortness slam.
S 4=5=1=3 answers 3D (singleton D); cue tail then signs off 5H. Clean ask, no slam values.
S 5=1=3=4 18 HCP but answers 3H — shortness takes priority over the strong-balanced answer. RKCB to 6S.
S 6=3=2=2 19 bal answers 3S (18+, no shortness) — the rare strong-balanced step. Cues, signs off 5S.
S 4=5=2=2 13 bal answers 4H (minimum, no shortness); N passes. Clean.
S 6=2=2=3 12 answers 4S (minimum, no shortness); N passes. Clean minimum signoff.
S 5=3=1=4 answers 3D (singleton D) correctly; very long cue/RKCB tail to 6S.
S 5=1=3=4 answers 3H (singleton H); N has no slam interest and signs off 4S.
S 1=5=4=3 answers 3S (singleton S) correctly; N's RKCB tail reaches 7H — aggressive grand (DD down). 6H safer.
S 1=5=2=5 answers 4C — the rare good-5-card-side-suit step (AK954). Cue/RKCB to 6H. Convention well shown.
S 2=5=5=1 answers 4D (good 5-card D side suit, AKJ54); RKCB to 6H making 12.
S 5=3=1=4 answers 3D (singleton D); RKCB finds a key missing, signs off 5S.
S 4=5=3=1 answers 3C (singleton C); cue tail then RKCB signs off 5H short of slam.
S 1=5=3=4 has a singleton S but answers 4H (minimum, no shortness) — suppresses the shortness Jacoby exists to show.
S 5=3=1=4 answers 3D (singleton D); cue/RKCB tail, N pushes 5S to 6S making 11.
S 5=1=4=3 answers 3H (singleton H); cue exchange shows no slam, signs off 4S.
S 5=0=5=3: 4D shows the good 5-card D side suit; 3H (void H shortness) equally valid. RKCB to 6S making 13.
S 5=1=5=2 answers 3H (singleton H); long cue/RKCB tail signs off 5S short of slam.
S 6=1=4=2 answers 3H (singleton H); cue and RKCB to 6S making 13 opposite N's 21. Clean.
S 5=1=3=4 answers 3H (singleton H); N jumps 4S then RKCB tail to 6S making 12.
S 4=5=3=1 answers 3C (singleton C) correctly; N drives to 6H — thin (DD down). Signing off in 5H defensible.
S 5=2=4=2 13 bal answers 4S (minimum, no shortness); N passes. Clean.
S 5=1=5=2 answers 4D (good 5-card D side suit, AK853); RKCB finds a key missing, signs off 5S.
S 3=5=3=2 14 bal answers 3NT (no shortness); N has the values and jumps to 6H making 12.
S 6=3=2=2 13 answers 4S (minimum); N RKCBs to 6S making 12. Clean minimum-then-N-drives.
S 5=1=4=3 answers 3H (singleton H); long cue/RKCB tail signs off 5S short of slam.
S 4=5=1=3 answers 3D (singleton D); very long cue/RKCB tail, N pushes to 6H making 12.
S 5=5=2=1: 3C shows singleton C; 4H (good 5-card H side suit) also valid with 5-5. Cue/RKCB to 6S making 13.
S 0=5=4=4 answers 3S (void S) correctly; long tail to 7H — aggressive grand (DD down). 6H the safe stop.
S 2=5=3=3 13 bal answers 4H (minimum, no shortness); N passes. Clean signoff.
S 5=3=2=3 12 bal answers 4S (minimum, no shortness); N passes. Clean.
S opens 1H, 12 HCP balanced 5H no shortness; 4H = minimum signoff. Clean, simplest J2NT answer.
3S = spade void; N cooperates, 4N RKC, 5D, 6H. Shortness answer drives a sound slam exploration.
3D = singleton D, sound. 4N RKC, 5C shows 1 keycard, signs off 5S; minor wrinkle is the missing keycard tail.
3H = heart singleton; cue 4C/4D exchange then sign off 4S. Clean shortness-then-cue auction.
3N = 15-17 balanced no shortness, correct. Cue sequence + 4N RKC to 6S; long but well-judged slam.
S 12 HCP balanced 5S no shortness; 4S = minimum signoff. Clean minimum answer.
S has 14 5332; 3N (15-17 bal) slightly overstates. N jumps to 6H off a control. Defensible but answer light.
S 11 HCP 5S no shortness; 4S = minimum signoff. Textbook minimum answer.
3N = 15-17 bal (14 borderline); cue 4C, 4N RKC to 6S. Slightly aggressive 3N but slam is right.
3C = club singleton; 3H sign, cue 4C/4D, settle 4H. Clean shortness answer, game reached.
3N = 15-17 bal (14, no shortness) acceptable; cue 4C then 4H. Both minimum, sensible game stop.
3C = club singleton; cue 4D/4H, 4N RKC, 5H, sign 5S off a key. Minor wrinkle: stops in 5S.
3C = club singleton; 4N RKC, 5H, 5S off a keycard. Sound shortness answer, slam correctly declined.
3N = 15-17 bal (14, no shortness); cue 4C, sign 4H. Both minimum, clean game stop.
3D = diamond singleton; 3H sign, 4N RKC, 5H, 6H. Shortness answer drives a clean slam.
S 12 HCP 5H no shortness; 4H = minimum signoff opposite 17. Clean minimum answer.
3S = spade shortness; long cue sequence + 4N to 5H, slam declined off two keys. Defensible, slightly long.
3N = 14 bal no shortness; N (18) jumps to 6S on power. Direct leap skips cue/RKC but slam is right.
S 13 HCP 6S no shortness; 4S = minimum signoff. Clean minimum answer.
3H = heart singleton; cue 4C/4D, sign 4S with two minimums. Clean shortness then settle in game.
3N = 14 bal no shortness; cue 4D/4H, 4N RKC, 5C, sign 5S off a key. Sound, slam correctly stopped.
4C = good 5-card club suit; N cues 4D, 4N RKC, 5C, 5S off a key. Clear second-suit answer.
3H = 18+ no shortness (17, strong bal); N drives cues + 4N to 6H. Slightly long but well-judged slam.
3C = club singleton; 3S sign, cue 4H, 4N RKC, 5H, 6S. Shortness answer drives a clean slam.
3C = club singleton; 3H sign, 4N RKC, 5H, 5N kings, 6D, 6H. Long Blackwood tail but well-controlled.
3C club shortness then 3D shows extra shape; 3H/4H settle in game. Clean light-minimum sequence.
3D = diamond singleton; 3H, 4N RKC, 5D, 5N kings, 6C, 7H. Shortness answer powers an accurate grand.
S 12 HCP 5H no shortness; 4H = minimum signoff. Clean minimum answer.
3D = diamond singleton; very long cue/RKC tail (4N,5D,5H,6D) lands 6S. Sound but meandering.
3D = diamond singleton; N signs off 4S with no slam interest. Clean shortness then stop in game.
S 13 HCP 5S no shortness; 4S = minimum signoff. Clean minimum answer.
S 11 HCP 5S no shortness; 4S = minimum signoff. Clean minimum answer.
3C = club singleton; 3S sign, 4N RKC, 5C, 5D queen-ask, 5H, 6S. Shortness drives a clean slam.
3C = club shortness (void); 3H, 4C cue, 4N RKC, 5S, 6H. Shortness answer drives a clean slam.
3H = 18 strong bal no shortness; N 4N RKC, 5D, 5S, 5N kings, 6H. Sound, slightly long king tail.
S 13 HCP 5S no shortness; 4S = minimum signoff opposite 17. Clean minimum answer.
3C = club singleton; 3S, cue 4C/4D, 4N RKC, 5H, 6S. Shortness answer drives a clean slam.
3S = spade shortness; 3N, 4N RKC, 5H, stops in 5H off a key. Sound shortness answer, slam declined.
3N = 14 bal no shortness; cue 4C/4D/4H, 4N RKC, 5H, 5S off a key. Sound, slam correctly stopped.
S 13 HCP 5H no shortness; 4H = minimum signoff. Clean minimum answer.
S 12 HCP 6H no shortness; 4H = minimum signoff opposite 18. Clean minimum answer.
S 12 HCP 5S no shortness; 4S = minimum signoff. Clean minimum answer.
3H = heart singleton; 3S, N signs off 4S. Clean shortness answer, sensible game stop.
3S = spade singleton; 4N RKC, 5S, 6H. Shortness answer drives a clean slam exploration.
3N = 14 bal no shortness; cue 4C/4D, sign 4S. Sound, both stop in game with no extra.
3C = club singleton; long cue + late 4N to 5S off a key. Auction wanders but each call defensible.
S 11 HCP 5H no shortness; 4H = minimum signoff. Clean minimum answer.
3D = diamond singleton; 3S, 4N RKC, 5C, 5S off a key. Sound shortness answer, slam correctly declined.
S opens 1H, 3D shows singleton D, then RKCB to 6H. Clean shortness answer + slam, makes 13.
3S claims 18+ strong-balanced but S has only 16 HCP; aggressive answer. 6S still makes 12. 4S minimum-ish answer defensible.
S opens 1H, 3D shows singleton D, RKCB to 6H. Clean shortness answer, makes 12.
S opens 1S, 3D shows void D, RKCB to 6S. Textbook shortness answer + slam, makes 12.
3D singleton answer correct; 6H on values via RKCB but DD down 1 (bad break). Signing off 4H also defensible on 15 HCP.
S opens 1H with 11 HCP minimum, 4H = minimum no shortness no slam. Clean signoff, makes 11.
S opens 1S 13 HCP, 4S = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff; N's 18 not enough alone, makes 12.
S opens 1S 13 HCP, 4S = minimum no shortness. Correct signoff, makes 11.
S 18 HCP no shortness; 3H = strong balanced answer correct, then cue 4C/4D and RKCB to 6H. DD down 1 (bad luck). Long but instructive.
S opens 1H 11 HCP, 4H = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 11.
S opens 1S, 3H = good 5-card... actually 3H here a side-suit feature; N retreats 3S, 4S signoff. Sound, no slam, makes 11.
S opens 1S, 3C shows singleton C (has 1). Correct shortness; N has no slam, 3S/4S signoff, makes 11.
S opens 1S, 3N = balanced 15-17 no shortness; N has 15 + AKx, drives 6S. Clean, makes 12.
S opens 1H, 3C singleton C; cue 4D, RKCB to 6H. Clean shortness answer + slam, makes 12.
S opens 1S, 3D singleton D correct; long cue/RKCB/5N tail to 6S. Sound but a wordy Blackwood sequence, makes 12.
S opens 1H, 3C singleton C; cue 4D, RKCB to 6H. Clean shortness answer + slam, makes 12.
S opens 1S, 3N = balanced 15-17 no shortness; N min, signs off 4S. Clean, makes 11.
S opens 1S, 3C then 3D shows shortness/shape; RKCB to 6S but DD only 11 (down 1, bad break). Stopping 5S defensible.
S opens 1S 13 HCP, 4S = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 11.
S opens 1S, 3N balanced 15-17; N probes (4D cue) then RKCB after E's X, stops 5S off a key card. Reasonable, makes 11.
S opens 1H, 3S shows void S (has void). Correct shortness; N RKCB to 6H, makes 12.
S opens 1S 12 HCP, 4S = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 10 exactly.
S opens 1S 11 HCP, 4S = minimum; N has 19, RKCB 4N to 6S. Clean min-answer override, makes 12.
S opens 1H, 3S shows void/singleton S (singleton A); 3N, RKCB to 6H. Clean shortness + slam, makes 13.
S opens 1H, 3D singleton D (has 1). Correct shortness; cue 4C, signs off 4H, makes 10. Sound stop.
S opens 1S, 4S = minimum no shortness; N drives RKCB/6D to 6S. Aggressive slam on combined 27, makes 12.
S opens 1S 18 HCP, 3S = strong balanced no shortness; very long cue/RKCB/5N tail to 6S. Sound but wordy, makes 13.
S opens 1S, 3D singleton D (has 1). Correct shortness; N cue 4H, signs off 4S, makes 12. Sound.
S opens 1H, 3C singleton C; cue 4D/4S, RKCB to 6H. Clean shortness answer + slam, makes 12.

S opens 1S, 3N = balanced 15-17 no shortness; N cue 4C, signs off 4S. Sound, makes 11.
S opens 1H 12 HCP, 4H = minimum no shortness; but N has 18 balanced — 6H/slam try possible. Stopping 4H defensible, makes 11.
S opens 1H 12 HCP, 4H = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 10 exactly.
S opens 1H, 3N = balanced 15-17; N leaps 6H on 14+15 combined. Reasonable slam but DD down 1 (bad break). Stopping game defensible.
S opens 1H, 4D = good 5-card D side suit; N RKCB to 6H. Clean second-suit answer + slam, makes 13.
S opens 1S 13 HCP, 4S = minimum no shortness; N 18 but balanced no slam, passes. Clean, makes 12.
S opens 1H, 3N = balanced 15-17; N cue 4C, RKCB to 6H. Clean strong-balanced answer + slam, makes 13.
S opens 1H 13 HCP, 4H = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 10 exactly.
S opens 1H 13 HCP, 4H = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 10 exactly.
S opens 1H 13 HCP, 4H = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 10 exactly... result 11.
S opens 1S, 3C singleton C (has 1); cue 4D, RKCB to 6S. Clean shortness answer + slam, makes 12.
S opens 1S, 3N = balanced 15-17; N cue 4D, S cue 4H, signs off 4S. Sound, no slam, makes 11.
S opens 1S, 3C singleton C (has 1). Correct shortness; N min, 3S/4S signoff, makes 11.
S opens 1S 19 HCP, 3D singleton... has 4 D, so good 5-card... 3D=shortness; RKCB+5N grand-slam ask to 7S, makes 13.
S opens 1H 12 HCP, 4H = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 10... result 11.
S opens 1H 13 HCP, 4H = minimum no shortness. Clean signoff, makes 11.
S opens 1S, 3N = balanced 15-17 no shortness; N min, signs off 4S. Clean, makes 11.
S opens 1H, 3D singleton D (has 1). Correct shortness; 4H then N RKCB to 6H, makes 12.
1S-2N; opener 4D = good 5-card D side suit (AKJ85), partner RKC, 6S on 12 tricks. Clean second-suit answer into slam.
1S-2N; opener 3H = H singleton (stiff K), correct. Long cue/RKC tail to 6S, DD down both. Right answer, aggressive slam push.
1S-2N; opener 4S = minimum (12), no shortness, no slam interest. Textbook signoff in game.
1S-2N; opener 3D = D singleton (5 stiff). Cue exchange, no slam, signoff 4S. Clean shortness answer.
1S-2N; 3H = H singleton (stiff H7). RKC then 5S stops over missing key card. Correct shortness, prudent halt.
1S-2N; opener 4S = minimum (13), no shortness. Textbook signoff; North 17 has no extra to push.
1H-2N; opener 4H = minimum (13), no shortness, no slam interest. Clean signoff in game.
1S-2N; opener 3C = C singleton (stiff C2). Partner RKC, 6S on 12. Clean shortness answer drives the slam.
1S-2N; opener 3S = 18+ no shortness (16 here, balanced strong). Cue/RKC to 6S, makes 13. Strong-balanced answer.
1H-2N; 3S = S singleton (stiff A). RKC 4N, 5C shows, signoff 5H off a key card. Correct answer, sound halt.
1H-2N; opener 4C = good 5-card C side suit (AQ863). Cue, RKC, 6H makes. Clean second-suit answer.
1S-2N; opener 3C = C singleton (stiff T). RKC exchange to 6S, 18 opp 13, makes 12. Clean shortness then slam.
1S-2N; opener 4S = minimum (12), no shortness. Textbook signoff in game.
1S-2N; opener 4S = minimum (12), no shortness, no slam try. Clean signoff even though N has 18.
1H-2N; opener 4H = minimum (12), no shortness. Clean signoff; makes 13 on the lie but bidding is sound.
1H-2N; opener 3N = balanced ~15-17 (14, no shortness). Partner cue 4C, signoff 4H. Minor wrinkle: 3N answer then game.
1H-2N; opener 3N = balanced 15-17 (15). Partner RKC, exchange to 6H, makes 12. Clean balanced answer into slam.
1S-2N; opener 3C = C singleton (stiff K). Cue 4C, RKC, halt 5S off a key card. Correct shortness answer, sound stop.
1H-2N; 3C = C singleton (stiff T). Cue 4D/4S, RKC, 6H makes 12. Correct shortness answer, full slam exploration.
1S-2N; opener 3N = balanced 15-17 (14, no shortness). Cue 4D/4H then signoff 4S. Sound, slight 3N overstatement at 14.
1H-2N; 3N = balanced 15-17 (15). RKC, 6H reached, DD down both. Slam push is aggressive on the lie but defensible.
1S-2N; opener 4S = minimum (12), no shortness. Textbook signoff in game.
1S-2N; opener 3H = good 4-5 H but here KQ32... 3H = H shortness? S has J stiff. Cue 4C then signoff 4S, no slam.
1S-2N; opener 4D = good 5-card D side suit (AQJT2). RKC 4N, 6S makes 12. Clean second-suit answer to slam.
1H-2N; 3D = D singleton (stiff 7). Cue 4C, RKC, halt 5H off a key card. Correct shortness, sound slam-or-game call.
1H-2N; opener 4H = minimum (13) no shortness. Partner RKC anyway with 18, exchange to 6H. Long Blackwood tail.
1S-2N; opener 4S = minimum (13), no shortness. Textbook signoff in game.
1S-2N; opener 4D = good 5-card D side suit (AKJ95). Partner signs 4S, then RKC, halt 5S off a key card. Sound.
1H-2N; opener 4H = minimum (12), no shortness. North 20 drives RKC then 6H. Clean minimum answer; N takes over.
1H-2N; opener 3D = D singleton... S has stiff? S=A in D. RKC 4N, 6H on 18 opp 12, makes 12. Clean answer to slam.
1H-2N; 3S = S singleton (stiff 6). 3N relay, RKC, 6H makes 12. Correct shortness answer, full slam auction.
1S-2N; opener 3N = balanced 15-17 (14, no shortness). Partner signs off 4S. Sound; 3N slight overbid at 14.
1H-2N; opener 3D = D shortness (void). E doubles, partner jumps 4H. Correct void-showing answer; competition truncates.
1H-2N; opener 3H = 18+ no shortness (19, strong balanced). Partner RKC, 6H makes 12. Strong-balanced answer to slam.
1H-2N; opener 3S = S singleton (stiff J). Cue 4C, RKC, 6H makes 13. Clean shortness answer driving the slam.
1S-2N; opener 4D = good 5-card D (AJ972). Very long cue/RKC tail to 6S, makes 12. Sound but runaway-looking exploration.
1H-2N; 3D = D singleton (stiff 9). Cue 4C, RKC, 6H reached, DD down both. Aggressive slam on the lie but defensible.
1H-2N; 3D = D singleton (stiff 4). RKC, 6H reached, DD down both. Slam push aggressive on this layout but defensible.
1S-2N; opener 3C = C singleton (stiff J), correct. RKC then 5N king-ask, 6S makes 12. Right answer, very long Blackwood tail.
1S-2N; opener 4S = minimum (11), no shortness, no slam interest. Textbook signoff in game.
1H-2N; opener 4H = minimum (12), no shortness. Textbook signoff in game.
1H-2N; opener 3D = D singleton (stiff K), correct. Long cue/RKC tail to 6H, makes 12. Right shortness answer, murky-looking continuation.
1S-2N; opener 3H = H singleton (stiff 2). Cue 4D/4H then signoff 4S, no slam. Clean shortness answer, prudent halt.
1S-2N; 3C = C singleton (stiff A). Cue, RKC, very long tail to 6S, makes 12. Correct shortness; runaway-looking auction.
1H-2N; opener 4H = minimum (13), no shortness. Textbook signoff in game.
1S-2N; opener 3N = balanced 15-17 (14, no shortness). Partner signs off 4S. Sound; 3N slight overstatement at 14.
1H-2N; opener 3D = D singleton (stiff K). Cue 4D/4S, RKC, 6H makes 13. Clean shortness answer, full slam auction.
1S-2N; opener 4S = minimum (11), no shortness, no slam interest. Textbook signoff in game.
S opens 1S; 3H correctly shows the stiff H. RKCB finds a key missing and the partnership stops in 5S. Long control tail.
S has 16 5-2-4-2 yet jumps to 3H (the 18+ strong answer). 3N (15-17 bal) better describes the values; 6H is DD-down.
S opens 1S; 3H correctly shows the stiff H (Q). Controls cued, RKCB shows a key missing, stops in 5S. Sound, long tail.
3H correctly shows the stiff H. A long cue/RKCB exchange settles in 5S off a key. Bidding fine; wrong-sided DD is a play matter.
S opens 1S with 18 balanced; 3S is the strong 18+ no-shortness answer. Cue, RKCB, 6S. Convention on clear display.
S opens 1H; 3C correctly shows the stiff C. Blackwood then 6H. Clean shortness-showing Jacoby auction.
S has a stiff C but jumps to 4S (minimum, no shortness), hiding it. With only 11 HCP signing off is defensible, but 3C is more informative.
S opens 1S; 3D correctly shows the stiff D (K). Long cue/RKCB tail to 6S. Sound.
S opens 1S with 12 balanced; 4S is the minimum no-shortness answer. N has no slam, passes. Clean signoff.
S opens 1S with 13 no shortness; 4S minimum answer, N passes. Textbook minimum Jacoby.
S opens 1H; 3S correctly shows the stiff S (K). Long Blackwood/control tail to 6H. Answer correct; 6H DD-down is a play matter.
S opens 1H; 4C shows the good 5-card C side suit. RKCB then 6H. Clean second-suit-showing answer.
